plugins.name: research-toolkit
plugins.description: Even-handed investigation of loaded, contested topics. Research & investigation skills for thinking-with-AI — investigation protocols (deep-investigation, cui-bono / "follow the money", screening a dev job or employer for hidden military/defense ties), text & source deconstruction, frame rotation, and iterative verification that escalates to a recursive adversarial dialectic (generate a claim's exact opposite and test it). The skepticism is symmetric — turned on the consensus, the contrarian take, the model's own bias, and its own conclusions; it assumes no villain in advance (POSIWID: judge a method by what it does, not what it claims). Includes a transparency MCP.

plugins.name: makers-toolkit
plugins.description: Discipline for prompting and co-authoring with AI — nothing to configure, just skills. Giving the model the reason behind a rule, not just the rule, clearly helps (anecdotally so far); the bolder idea behind it — leaving room for the model's own agency — is the core experimental proposition. Plus engineering discipline for deterministic systems and a test for whether a skill description actually fires.

plugins.name: vasana-system
plugins.description: A working proof of concept for catching the same structure in places that have nothing to do with each other — and in your own working process: notice such a regularity, capture it, and test each for a real shared mechanism, not surface resemblance. Today it's a growing, hand-kept list of specific observations that resurface when the shape shows up again, each accumulating a count of the times it has recurred. The self-organizing learner the system is built toward is the genuine direction, not yet the state — an early, already-useful step toward it. Memory for forms, not just facts; two local MCP servers (relational-memory + a usage-weighted knowledge graph, experimental). The term "vasana" is grounded in the plugin's axioms.

plugins.name: security-toolkit
plugins.description: Useful friction against obvious mistakes — a basic draft, not a security product. Threat-detection and dangerous-action-blocking hooks, plus security skills: tiered prompt-injection detection with allowlist, dangerous git blocking (push to main, force push, reset --hard, etc.), Desktop Commander safeguards, an optional off-by-default PR-merge guard (a human always merges, toggled via /pr-merge-guard), and a guided "am I compromised?" IOC triage + attack-surface reduction for Windows + WSL2 dev boxes after a supply-chain scare (verifies, does not harden; a clean result raises confidence, never proves).
